preamble: The open-mid front unrounded vowel [ɛ] shifts toward the open-mid back unrounded vowel [ʌ] through backing in the Northern Cities Vowel Shift (NCVS), a major chain shift affecting American English in the Great Lakes region and Inland North. The NCVS is an ongoing, well-documented dialect change that began in the early 20th century and is characterized by coordinated shifts in the vowel space that maintain phonemic contrast.

examples.note: DRESS vowel in words like 'bet', 'dress', 'met' backs toward or overlaps with STRUT vowel position in Northern Cities Shift
In speakers with advanced Northern Cities Shift, DRESS vowel backs and can approach STRUT vowel quality
Extremes of the Northern Cities Shift show DRESS vowel shifting toward or merging with STRUT vowel position in vowel space

languageExamples.language: Inland Northern American English (Buffalo, Cleveland, Detroit, Chicago)

examples.note: Regional dialect shift most strongly present in urban centers of Great Lakes region; part of larger chain shift affecting six vowels
